 {"id":"bd-10qa5","title":"CAAM: Add [integrations.caam] config section to TOML","description":"Add configuration support for CAAM integration in NTM config file.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.caam]
# Enable CAAM account management
enabled = true

# Path to caam binary (optional, defaults to PATH lookup)
binary_path = ""

# Enable automatic account rotation on rate limit
auto_rotate = true

# Providers to manage (empty = all available)
providers = ["claude", "openai", "gemini"]

# Rate limit detection patterns (in addition to defaults)
rate_limit_patterns = []

# Cooldown before retrying same account (seconds)
account_cooldown = 300

# Alert threshold (percentage of limit)
alert_threshold = 80
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go (add CAAMConfig struct)
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go (set defaults)

## Struct Definition

```go
type CAAMConfig struct {
    Enabled           bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath        string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    AutoRotate        bool     `toml:"auto_rotate"`
    Providers         []string `toml:"providers"`
    RateLimitPatterns []string `toml:"rate_limit_patterns"`
    AccountCooldown   int      `toml:"account_cooldown"`
    AlertThreshold    int      `toml:"alert_threshold"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:30.659313954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:57.907305322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:53.391877677Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10qa5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:30.731389875Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-10ufs","title":"EPIC: Fleet Management Commands (scale, rebalance, review-queue)","description":"## Goal
Provide a cohesive set of commands for managing agent fleet composition, workload distribution, and batch operations.

## Commands in Scope

### ntm scale
Manual scaling to target agent counts without autonomous auto-scaling.
- `ntm scale <session> --cc=N --cod=N --gmi=N`
- Computes delta vs current counts and calls ntm add/kill
- Safe confirmations for scale-down
- Never auto-kills user pane

### ntm rebalance
Manual rebalance suggestions when agents join/leave or workloads become uneven.
- `ntm rebalance <session>` shows imbalance and suggests reassignments
- `ntm rebalance <session> --apply` applies after confirmation
- Detects dead agents and suggests orphan task reassignment

### ntm review-queue
Suggests review prompts for idle agents to keep them productive.
- `ntm review-queue <session>` lists idle agents + recommended prompts
- `ntm review-queue <session> --send` dispatches after confirmation
- Configurable idle threshold and review templates

### ntm send --batch
Explicit batch prompt delivery from files.
- `ntm send <session> --batch file.txt` sends prompts in order
- Supports comments (#) and separators (---) in batch files
- Agent targeting: --filter, --agent, --broadcast

## Design Philosophy
- All commands are **manual** (user-initiated), not autonomous
- All modifications require **confirmation** (unless --force)
- All commands support **--dry-run** for safe preview
- All commands support **--format json** for robot mode
- Graceful degradation when assignment tracking unavailable

## Success Criteria
- Commands work without assignment tracking (basic mode)
- Commands enhance with assignment tracking when available
- Clear, deterministic output for same input state
- No background daemons or automatic triggers
- Comprehensive logging for debugging","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-11mtt","title":"Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset","description":"# Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset

## Purpose
Ship the **complete 80-mode taxonomy** from `modes_of_reasoning.md` in `internal/ensemble/modes.go`, with tiered visibility for UX filtering. The catalog must be fully usable without external files.

- **core tier (~28)**: curated default set for UX
- **advanced tier (remaining modes)**: hidden by default but fully accessible via `--all` / `--tier`

## Core Tier (Curated 28)

> These are `tier: core` and must have complete metadata + hand-authored preambles.
> **Mode codes and categories must match the numbering in `modes_of_reasoning.md` (A–L).**
> The groupings below are advisory; final category assignment must follow the doc.

- deductive — Deductive Inference
- mathematical-proof — Mathematical Proof
- formal-verification — Formal Verification
- type-theoretic — Type-Theoretic Reasoning
- inductive — Inductive Generalization
- conceptual-blending — Conceptual Blending
- analogical — Analogical Transfer
- ambiguity-detection — Ambiguity Detection
- edge-case — Edge Case Reasoning
- prototype-reasoning — Prototype Reasoning
- root-cause — Root Cause Analysis
- dependency-mapping — Dependency Mapping
- argument-mapping — Argument Mapping
- adversarial-review — Adversarial Review
- systems-thinking — Systems Thinking
- worst-case — Worst-Case Analysis
- decision-under-uncertainty — Decision Under Uncertainty
- failure-mode — Failure Mode Analysis
- second-order-effects — Second-Order Effects
- resource-allocation — Resource Allocation
- strategic-planning — Strategic Planning
- option-generation — Option Generation
- prioritization — Prioritization
- engineering-design — Engineering Design Reasoning
- test-plan — Test Plan Mode
- compliance — Compliance Lens
- meta-cognitive — Meta-cognitive Monitoring
- perspective-taking — Perspective Taking

## Advanced Tier (Remaining 52)

- All remaining modes from `modes_of_reasoning.md` are included as `tier: advanced`.
- Categories must remain within A–L taxonomy.
- ModeCode **letter must match category** (A→Formal, B→Ampliative, ... L→Meta).

## Implementation Pattern
```go
var EmbeddedModes = []ReasoningMode{
    {
        ID:        "deductive",
        Code:      "A1",
        Tier:      TierCore,
        Name:      "Deductive Inference",
        Category:  CategoryFormal,
        ShortDesc: "Classical logical inference",
        // ...
    },
    // ... all 80 modes
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **All 80 modes** defined with complete metadata
- [ ] Core tier is exactly the curated 28 list above
- [ ] Advanced modes included and validated (no feature loss)
- [ ] Codes unique and category letters correct
- [ ] Categories limited to A–L taxonomy
- [ ] Modes validated at init time","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:09.088788198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T10:22:06.842533822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:01.350316238Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:33:43.770208854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11y0m","title":"LimitDetector: Capture pane output via tmux capture-pane","description":"## LimitDetector: Capture Pane Output via tmux capture-pane

### Purpose
Implement the pane output capture functionality that feeds pattern matching.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Function:**
```go
// capturePaneOutput captures the last N lines from a pane.
func (d *LimitDetector) capturePaneOutput(sessionPane string, lines int) (string, error) {
    // sessionPane format: "session_name:window.pane" (e.g., "cc_agents_1:1.3")
    
    // Use /usr/bin/tmux to avoid zsh plugin issues
    args := []string{
        "capture-pane",
        "-t", sessionPane,
        "-p",                          // Print to stdout
        fmt.Sprintf("-S", "-%d", lines), // Start line (negative = from end)
    }
    
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "/usr/bin/tmux", args...)
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("capture-pane failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    return string(output), nil
}
```

**Integration with Existing Code:**
The `internal/tmux/session.go` already has capture-pane functionality. Reuse that:
```go
output, err := tmux.CapturePaneOutput(sessionPane, 50)
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log capture: `[LimitDetector] capture_pane session_pane=%s lines=%d bytes=%d`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capture last N lines from pane
- [ ] Proper session:window.pane addressing
- [ ] Use /usr/bin/tmux directly
- [ ] Handle capture failures gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:54.713444511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427395555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427347334Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.392125380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-rt9vb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.149105820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2fov1","title":"Task: Implement synthesis output formatter (markdown/json/yaml)","description":"# Task: Implement synthesis output formatter (markdown/json/yaml)

Provide consistent formatting of SynthesisResult for CLI, robot mode, and file export.

## Supported Formats

### 1. Markdown (default)
```markdown
# Ensemble Synthesis Report

**Problem:** [Problem statement]
**Ensemble:** [preset or explicit modes]
**Strategy:** [synthesis strategy]
**Date:** [timestamp]

## Executive Summary
...

## Perspectives
### [Mode Name] ([Mode ID / Code])
- Key insights
- Risks
- Recommendations

## Integrated Analysis
- Agreements
- Disagreements
- Novel insights

## Recommendations (ranked)
| Priority | Recommendation | Rationale | Effort |
|----------|---------------|-----------|--------|
| 1        | ...           | ...       | ...    |

## Appendix
- Raw outputs (optional)
- Metrics (coverage, redundancy, velocity)
```

### 2. JSON
```json
{
  "meta": {"problem": "...", "ensemble": "...", "strategy": "...", "timestamp": "..."},
  "summary": "...",
  "perspectives": [...],
  "analysis": {"agreements": [...], "disagreements": [...], "novel_insights": [...]},
  "recommendations": [...],
  "metrics": {...}
}
```

### 3. YAML
Same structure as JSON, human-readable.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/formatter.go
type OutputFormat string

const (
    FormatMarkdown OutputFormat = "markdown"
    FormatJSON     OutputFormat = "json"
    FormatYAML     OutputFormat = "yaml"
)

type SynthesisFormatter struct {
    Format OutputFormat
}

func (f *SynthesisFormatter) Format(result *SynthesisResult) (string, error)
func (f *SynthesisFormatter) FormatMarkdown(result *SynthesisResult) string
func (f *SynthesisFormatter) FormatJSON(result *SynthesisResult) (string, error)
func (f *SynthesisFormatter) FormatYAML(result *SynthesisResult) (string, error)
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/formatter.go

## Dependencies
- Uses SynthesisResult from synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)
- Used by CLI synthesize command (bd-3c0qv)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All formats stable and deterministic
- [ ] Markdown is human-readable and consistent
- [ ] JSON/YAML schemas documented
- [ ] Formatter unit tests cover all formats","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:04.508559713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T09:31:48.404959038Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fov1","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:31:48.404933830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fxyp","title":"Add ntm assign --pane for assigning specific bead to specific agent","description":"## Overview
Allow users to assign a specific bead to a specific agent pane directly.

## Use Cases
1. User knows which agent is best for a specific task
2. Testing/debugging with controlled assignments
3. Manually implementing a custom assignment strategy
4. Assigning high-priority bead to best-performing agent

## Usage
```bash
# Assign specific bead to specific pane
ntm assign myproject --pane=3 bd-xyz

# With custom prompt
ntm assign myproject --pane=3 bd-xyz --prompt="Focus on the API changes"

# Multiple beads to one pane (queued)
ntm assign myproject --pane=3 bd-xyz,bd-abc

# Bypass strategy entirely
ntm assign myproject --pane=3 --bead=bd-xyz --no-strategy
```

## Workflow
1. Validate pane exists and is idle (or use --force)
2. Validate bead is assignable (not blocked, not already assigned)
3. Create assignment in store
4. Create file reservations via Agent Mail
5. Send prompt to agent
6. Return result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "pane",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "assignment": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 3,
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "agent_name": "GreenCastle",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z"
    },
    "file_reservations": ["src/api/*.ts"]
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Validation
- Pane must exist in session
- Bead must not be blocked (unless --ignore-deps)
- Bead must not already be assigned (unless --force)
- Agent should be idle (unless --force)

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "pane",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "BEAD_BLOCKED",
    "message": "bead bd-xyz is blocked by bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "blocked_by": ["bd-abc"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Pane doesn't exist in session
- BEAD_BLOCKED: Bead has unresolved dependencies
- ALREADY_ASSIGNED: Bead already assigned to another agent
- AGENT_BUSY: Target agent is working on another bead

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Assign specific bead to specific pane
- [ ] Validate pane exists
- [ ] Validate bead is assignable
- [ ] Support --force for override
- [ ] Support --ignore-deps for blocked beads
- [ ] Create file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Custom prompt support
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes and messages

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Validation logic
- Unit test: Force override behavior
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Direct pane assignment","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:15.139176140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:46.803147899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:46.803102864Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fxyp","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:09.698489234Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fxyp","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:09.570786017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fxyp","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:09.634793885Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2g6y","title":"Add NO_COLOR and TERM=dumb support for accessible plain mode","description":"## Problem
Plain theme may still show colors; need full NO_COLOR compliance.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: NO_COLOR Already Implemented

**theme.go:278-299** already handles NO_COLOR:
```go
// Respects the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/):
// - If NO_COLOR exists in environment (any value), colors are disabled
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=1 also disables colors
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=0 forces colors ON (overrides NO_COLOR)
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    ntmNoColor := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NTM_NO_COLOR"))
    ...
    _, noColorSet := os.LookupEnv("NO_COLOR")
    ...
}
```

**output/*.go** files also check NO_COLOR:
- text.go:189
- success.go:43
- errors.go:58
- confirm.go:53
- progress.go:56,201

### Missing: TERM=dumb Support

No handling found for TERM=dumb. This should be added:
```go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // Existing NO_COLOR checks...

    // Add TERM=dumb check
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### Missing: ASCII Fallback Verification

Need to verify Plain theme uses ASCII-only characters:
- Box drawing: +-| instead of Unicode
- Status icons: [OK] [FAIL] instead of ✓ ✗

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add TERM=dumb Check
```go
// theme.go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // ... existing checks ...

    // TERM=dumb should also disable colors
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### 2. Verify ASCII Icon Fallback
```go
// icons/icons.go
func asciiIcons() IconSet {
    return IconSet{
        Success: "[OK]",
        Error:   "[ERR]",
        Warning: "[WARN]",
        Info:    "[INFO]",
        // ... all ASCII
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTermDumbDisablesColor(t *testing.T) {
    t.Setenv("TERM", "dumb")
    t.Setenv("NTM_NO_COLOR", "")

    if !NoColorEnabled() {
        t.Error("TERM=dumb should enable no-color mode")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeNoANSI(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)

    styles := NewSemanticStyles(theme.Current())
    rendered := styles.TextSuccess.Render("test")

    if strings.Contains(rendered, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("Plain theme should not contain ANSI escape codes")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeASCIIOnly(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)
    icons := icons.Current()

    // All icons should be ASCII
    for name, icon := range icons.All() {
        for _, r := range icon {
            if r > 127 {
                t.Errorf("icon %s contains non-ASCII character: %c", name, r)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Verification
- [x] NO_COLOR=1 handled
- [x] NTM_NO_COLOR handled
- [ ] TERM=dumb handling - needs implementation

 {"id":"bd-2gnp3","title":"rano: Implement --robot-rano-stats command","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-rano-stats` command for per-agent network statistics via rano integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the established robot mode patterns:
- **Naming**: `--robot-rano-stats` (tool prefix: rano)
- **NOT** `--robot-network-stats` (must include tool name)
- **Modifiers**: shared `--panes=` flag

## Command Interface

```bash
# Get network stats for all agents
ntm --robot-rano-stats

# Filter to specific pane
ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=2,3

# Custom time window
ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=10m
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "window": "5m",
  "agents": {
    "pane_2": {
      "connections": 3,
      "bytes_sent": 45000,
      "bytes_received": 120000,
      "requests_estimate": 15,
      "providers": {
        "anthropic": {
          "connections": 2,
          "bytes_sent": 40000,
          "bytes_received": 100000
        }
      },
      "avg_latency_ms": 250
    }
  },
  "total": {
    "connections": 4,
    "bytes_sent": 55000,
    "bytes_received": 170000
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rano not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rano: go install github.com/.../rano@latest"
}
```

## Implementation

1. Check rano availability via ToolRegistry
2. Get PID map for all/specified panes via tmux
3. Call rano with PIDs: `rano --pids=PID1,PID2 --window=DURATION --json`
4. Aggregate stats by pane
5. Format as JSON

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--panes=N` | Filter to specific pane(s) | All agent panes |
| `--rano-window=DURATION` | Time window for stats | 5m |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rano_stats.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rano-stats` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--panes=N` filter works with comma-separated pane IDs
- [ ] `--rano-window=DURATION` accepts Go duration format (5m, 1h, etc.)
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rano returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping correctly attributes traffic
- [ ] Provider breakdown (anthropic/openai/google) works
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rano_stats_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping logic
- [ ] rano output parsing
- [ ] Provider aggregation
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rano
- [ ] Error handling for rano failures

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rano.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RANO] Robot Rano Stats Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RANO]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot rano E2E tests"

# Check if rano is available
if \! command -v rano &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rano not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Setup: Create test session
TEST_SESSION="e2e_rano_$$"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Creating test session: $TEST_SESSION"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5

# Test 1: Basic stats
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: Basic rano stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".timestamp") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: Pane filter
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: Pane-filtered stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=1)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Custom window
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Custom time window"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=1m)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".window") == "1m" ]] || exit 1

# Cleanup
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot rano E2E tests passed"
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:15:41.491769242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T17:53:10.682661963Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xxhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:18:14.046829499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:18:33.160435095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-308f5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:15:41.596388073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-33wzo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:17:54.645985410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gp6","title":"Fix ANSI escape code width calculation errors","description":"## Problem
Width calculations not properly accounting for ANSI escape codes, causing misalignment and overflow.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

**Good news: The codebase already handles ANSI width correctly in most places.**

### Already Correct:
- `internal/cli/table.go:208` - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width(s)`
- `internal/tui/layout/layout.go:102` - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`
- `internal/tui/styles/badges.go:687` - Documents: "Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes"
- `internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go` - Builds plain text first, then styles visible portion
- 36+ uses of `lipgloss.Width()` found across the codebase

### Remaining Audit Needed
Verify no edge cases where `len()` is used on strings that might already be styled:
1. Dashboard panel rendering code
2. Card grid layout calculations  
3. Status line construction
4. Any dynamic width calculations based on content

## Updated Fix Approach
1. **Audit** all width calculations - verify they use lipgloss.Width() or operate on plain text
2. **Add tests** to verify styled string width handling
3. **Document** the correct pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestANSIWidthCalculations(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify lipgloss.Width() correctly ignores ANSI codes
    plain := "Hello"
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("Hello")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != lipgloss.Width(plain) {
        t.Errorf("styled width %d != plain width %d", lipgloss.Width(styled), lipgloss.Width(plain))
    }
    
    // Verify runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
    w := runeWidth(styled)
    if w != 5 {
        t.Errorf("runeWidth(styled) = %d, want 5", w)
    }
}

func TestGradientTextWidth(t *testing.T) {
    text := "Test"
    gradient := styles.GradientText(text, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    // Gradient text should have same display width as original
    if lipgloss.Width(gradient) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("gradient width = %d, want 4", lipgloss.Width(gradient))
    }
}

func TestTableColumnAlignment(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Name", "Value")
    // Add rows with styled content
    table.AddRow(lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Render("Bold"), "plain")
    table.AddRow("plain", lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("colored"))
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have consistent width (no jagged edges)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    // Check all widths are equal (within tolerance for borders)
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}
```

## Verification
- Run audit to confirm no `len()` used on styled strings for width
- All styled text aligns correctly in tables
- Gradient text does not cause overflow
- Unit tests pass

## Priority Adjustment
Changed from P1 to P2 - most code is already correct, this is mainly an audit/verification task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:28.352073676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711902846Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711841760Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: no len() on styled strings found. All width calculations use lipgloss.Width(). Added comprehensive unit tests in internal/cli/table_test.go covering ANSI width, styled strings, emoji, CJK, and table alignment.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gp6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:56.780144118Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gp6","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:41.552044686Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3s2f","title":"EPIC: Webhook System for External Integrations","description":"## Overview
Implement an event-driven webhook system that triggers HTTP callbacks on NTM events, enabling integration with Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, CI/CD systems, and custom automation.

## Problem Statement
NTM operates as a closed system. Users can't:
- Get notified in Slack when agents complete work
- Trigger CI pipelines when all agents finish
- Alert PagerDuty when errors occur
- Build custom dashboards from live data
- Integrate with existing DevOps tooling

## Key Features
1. Configurable webhook endpoints per event type
2. Built-in formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams
3. Retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Event filtering by session, agent type, severity
5. Payload templates with variable substitution

## Event Types
- agent.started: Agent session created
- agent.completed: Agent finished task
- agent.error: Agent encountered error
- agent.idle: Agent became idle
- session.created: New NTM session
- session.ended: Session terminated
- bead.assigned: Bead assigned to agent
- bead.completed: Bead marked done

## Configuration (ntm.yaml)
webhooks:
  - url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
    events: [agent.completed, agent.error]
    formatter: slack
    filter:
      session: myproject*
      severity: [warning, error, critical]
  - url: https://my-api.com/ntm-events
    events: [*]
    formatter: json

## Success Criteria
- Webhooks fire reliably on configured events
- Built-in formatters produce valid Slack/Discord messages
- Failures don't block NTM operation
- Easy to configure without code changes

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/webhook/manager.go (new - webhook orchestration)
- internal/webhook/formatters/ (new - Slack, Discord, JSON formatters)
- internal/config/webhooks.go (new - webhook config parsing)
- internal/events/emitter.go (event emission points)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3sha","title":"Task: Dashboard help respects minimal/full verbosity","description":"## Overview
Replace tier‑based dashboard hints with the **help verbosity** model (minimal/full) and optional debug mode, aligning with CLI help simplification.

## Deliverables
1. Update internal/tui/dashboard/help.go:
   - Minimal: essential keys only (navigate/select/quit)
   - Full: all standard keys (refresh/zoom/etc.)
   - Debug: add debug keys/panels when `--debug` or `NTM_DEBUG=1`

2. Status bar hint:
   - Show current help verbosity: `Help: minimal` or `Help: full`

3. Panel visibility:
   - Minimal: core panels (activity/status)
   - Full: all standard panels
   - Debug: include internal panels if available

## Implementation Notes
- Read verbosity from config at dashboard init
- Cache to avoid repeated file reads
- Update hints when verbosity changes (rare)

## Tests
- Unit: mapping of verbosity → key list
- E2E: dashboard rendering in minimal/full/debug with logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard respects help verbosity setting
- Keybinding hints filter appropriately
- Verbosity indicator shows in status bar
- E2E tests for minimal/full/debug rendering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:38.988102962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:51:55.266727384Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-352n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:45.264885044Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-vcmnv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:51:55.266711033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3u59m","title":"Implement AutoRespawner for limit-hit recovery","description":"## AutoRespawner: Core Interface for Agent Recovery

### Purpose
Implement the AutoRespawner that handles automatic agent recovery when usage limits are hit. Integrates with LimitDetector events and AccountRotator for seamless recovery.

### IMPORTANT: Leverage Existing Code
NTM already has `internal/context/rotation.go` with:
- PaneSpawner interface for creating/killing panes
- DefaultPaneSpawner implementation
- SendKeys for sending commands to panes

AutoRespawner should USE these existing abstractions, not duplicate them.

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// AutoRespawner handles automatic agent recovery.
type AutoRespawner struct {
    // Config holds respawn settings
    Config *config.SwarmConfig
    
    // LimitDetector provides limit events
    LimitDetector *LimitDetector
    
    // AccountRotator switches accounts (optional)
    AccountRotator *AccountRotator
    
    // PromptInjector re-sends marching orders
    PromptInjector *PromptInjector
    
    // PaneSpawner from context package (REUSE EXISTING)
    PaneSpawner context.PaneSpawner
    
    // eventChan emits respawn events
    eventChan chan RespawnEvent
}

// RespawnResult tracks a respawn attempt.
type RespawnResult struct {
    Success         bool          `json:"success"`
    SessionPane     string        `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType       string        `json:"agent_type"`
    AccountRotated  bool          `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string        `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string        `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
    Duration        time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    Error           string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
    RespawnedAt     time.Time     `json:"respawned_at"`
}
```

**Respawn Sequence:**
```
1. Receive LimitEvent from LimitDetector
2. Log respawn start
3. Kill stuck agent:
   a. Send /exit (for codex) or Ctrl+C twice (for claude)
   b. Wait 2s for graceful exit
   c. If still running, kill process via PID
4. (Optional) Rotate account via AccountRotator
5. Clear pane: tmux send-keys -t <pane> 'clear' Enter
6. Respawn agent: Send agent command (cc/cod/gmi)
7. Wait for agent ready (configurable delay, default 5s)
8. Re-inject marching orders via PromptInjector
9. Emit RespawnEvent
10. Log respawn complete
```

**Kill Sequences by Agent Type:**
```go
func (r *AutoRespawner) killAgent(sessionPane, agentType string) error {
    switch agentType {
    case "cc":
        // Claude: Double Ctrl+C with 100ms gap (CRITICAL timing)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    case "cod":
        // Codex: /exit command
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "/exit", true)
    case "gmi":
        // Gemini: Escape then Ctrl+C
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x1b", false)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    }
    return nil
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log respawn start: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_start session_pane=%s agent_type=%s`
- Log kill attempt: `[AutoRespawner] killing_agent session_pane=%s method=%s`
- Log account rotation: `[AutoRespawner] account_rotated old=%s new=%s`
- Log agent spawn: `[AutoRespawner] spawning_agent session_pane=%s`
- Log prompt injection: `[AutoRespawner] injecting_prompt session_pane=%s`
- Log respawn complete: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_complete session_pane=%s success=%t duration=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Uses existing PaneSpawner interface from context package
- [ ] Agent-specific kill sequences
- [ ] Configurable timeouts and delays
- [ ] Integration with AccountRotator (optional)
- [ ] Integration with PromptInjector
- [ ] Event emission for audit trail
- [ ] Max retry limits per pane
- [ ] Detailed structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:13.205417120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.538092929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.537904134Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: AutoRespawner with agent-specific kill sequences, configurable timeouts, AccountRotator integration, PromptInjector integration, event emission, max retry limits, and comprehensive logging. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-150ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:10.280363190Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1bruk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:10.323135973Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1v922","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:10.349604308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.440199986Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u5r","title":"E2E Tests: Agent Mail auto-registration on spawn","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of auto-registering agents with MCP Agent Mail on spawn.

## Test Scenario
1. Ensure Agent Mail server running
2. Spawn agents: --cc=2 --cod=1
3. Verify agents registered in Agent Mail
4. Verify correct agent names (adjective+noun)
5. Verify pane-to-name mapping stored

## Verification
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for registered agents
am agents list --project=/test/path

# Expected output shows 3 agents with names like:
# - GreenCastle (pane 2, claude)
# - BlueLake (pane 3, claude)
# - RedStone (pane 4, codex)
```

## Prerequisites
- Agent Mail MCP server running
- Test workspace created

## Logging
- Log registration API calls
- Log agent names assigned
- Log pane-to-name mapping
- Log any registration failures

## Test File
